articles.title: DISCOVERABILITY IS THE GAMING INDUSTRY'S "ACHILLES' HEEL," MARKETING SURVEY FINDS
articles.summary: Bastion's G2M Video Games Marketing Survey shows that discoverability is the biggest challenge for publishers and developers. 94.4% of respondents expect it to remain their top issue this year. Despite 63.9% of companies having over a decade of publishing experience, most have marketing teams of only 1-5 people, and just 36.1% believe their current budget is adequate. Budgets are tightening, and companies are shifting strategies, with many finding organic influencer marketing as the most effective channel and paid media as the least effective.

articles.title: AMAZON EXITS GOOGLE SHOPPING GLOBALLY CAUSING 30% CPC COLLAPSE
articles.summary: Amazon's exit from Google Shopping in July instantly reduced its presence from dominant market share to complete absence across the US, Europe, and Japan. This sudden change in auctions drove cost savings of about 25% to 30% for other advertisers and marked the company's boldest advertising strategy shift since its temporary withdrawal during the 2020 lockdowns.

articles.title: 5 SAAS GROWTH STRATEGIES THAT WORK
articles.summary: The Progressive Squeeze gradually increases upgrade prompts as users engage more, while the Feature Tease displays gated premium features to spark curiosity and FOMO. The Moment of Need tactic surfaces upgrade options when users are most invested, and the Transparent Countdown builds trust by clearly showing limits and resets. The Omnipresent Nudge integrates multiple contextual upgrade touchpoints across the interface.

articles.title: HOW TO MARKET ON REDDIT: WHY BRANDS FAIL AND HOW TO SUCCEED
articles.summary: With over 110M daily users, Reddit rewards genuine contributions and punishes anything staged. It's a trust-based economy where recommendations carry real weight. Brands like 1Password have succeeded by building communities and engaging transparently, while others face backlash for inauthentic tactics. Successful brands are led by people who understand internet culture, value transparency over control, and engage in unscripted conversations.

articles.title: GOOGLE ADS TO END MANUAL LANGUAGE TARGETING IN SEARCH CAMPAIGNS
articles.summary: Google Ads will remove manual language targeting for Search campaigns by year-end, shifting fully to AI-driven detection based on user signals. Non-Search campaigns like Display and YouTube remain unchanged. While this move simplifies setup and may improve targeting accuracy, it reduces advertiser control and risks misaligned placements.

articles.title: VIBE MARKETING STATISTICS – GROWTH & USAGE TRENDS
articles.summary: Searches for “vibe marketing” rose 686% in the past year, and nearly 47% of Fortune 500 companies now use it. VIBE stands for Velocity, Identity, Boundaryless, and Emotions first. Velocity means moving at cultural speed, Identity aligns campaigns with who consumers want to be, Boundaryless blends brand and product into a seamless experience, and Emotions first focuses on triggering specific feelings before crafting messages.

articles.title: IS YOUR FAVORITE SOCIAL MEDIA SITCOM ACTUALLY A MARKETING CAMPAIGN?
articles.summary: Bilt created the scripted series “Roomies” as a marketing campaign without featuring its brand in the first nine episodes. It has drawn 3.2M combined views on Instagram and TikTok without paid promotion. The company used a separate account for the show to align with social algorithms and avoid overtly promotional content. Bilt plans to eventually integrate its partners, such as Barry's gym, into the storyline. Other brands like Alexis Bittar, InStyle, and Sephora are also using episodic social content to connect with audiences, with some reporting strong community response and product sales tied to their series.

articles.title: BRAND ADVERTISING ON NEWS SITES ISN'T RISKY, IT'S ABSOLUTELY NECESSARY
articles.summary: Roughly 80M US adults closely follow the news, making them a highly desirable audience for advertisers. Studies show “news junkies” have 16 percentage points higher purchase intent than the general population and stronger trust and favorability toward brands. Yet many advertisers avoid news sites due to outdated brand safety fears, missing out on a demographic that includes most business leaders. Evidence shows ads in news perform just as well as those placed in entertainment, sports, or lifestyle content.

articles.title: 7 PRINCIPLES OF PERSUASION THAT MAKE YOUR GROWTH STRATEGY UNSTOPPABLE
articles.summary: 7 growth tactics to overcome buyer hesitation with psychology.

articles.title: 20 MARKETING AND COMMUNICATIONS TRENDS THAT AREN'T WORTH THE HYPE
articles.summary: 20 overrated trends that separate substance from hype.
